name: map_entry_addr_always_zero
reason: If provable, the conversion always loses the physical address, making all mappings point to address 0

name: map_entry_write_always_false
reason: If provable, write permission is never propagated to MapEntry, silently making all pages read-only

name: map_entry_xd_equals_write
reason: If provable, execute-disable and write are always conflated, preventing independent writable-but-executable or read-only-non-executable pages

name: l1_lookup_always_none
code:
proof fn phi_4_l1_lookup_always_none(pt: PageTable, l4i: L4Index, l3i: L3Index, l2i: L2Index, l1i: L1Index, l2_entry: PageEntry)
    requires
        pt.wf(),
        pt.kernel_l4_end <= l4i < 512,
        0 <= l3i < 512,
        0 <= l2i < 512,
        0 <= l1i < 512,
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l2(l4i, l3i, l2i) =~= Some(l2_entry),
    ensures
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_4k_l1(l4i, l3i, l2i, l1i).is_None(),
{
}
reason: If provable, no 4K page can ever be mapped through L1, rendering the entire 4K mapping layer vacuous

name: l1_mapping_always_readonly
code:
proof fn phi_5_l1_mapping_always_readonly(pt: PageTable, l4i: L4Index, l3i: L3Index, l2i: L2Index, l1i: L1Index, l2_entry: PageEntry)
    requires
        pt.wf(),
        pt.kernel_l4_end <= l4i < 512,
        0 <= l3i < 512,
        0 <= l2i < 512,
        0 <= l1i < 512,
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l2(l4i, l3i, l2i) =~= Some(l2_entry),
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_4k_l1(l4i, l3i, l2i, l1i).is_Some(),
    ensures
        pt.mapping_4k()[spec_index2va((l4i, l3i, l2i, l1i))].write == false,
{
}
reason: If provable, all resolved 4K mappings are forced read-only, meaning writable user pages can never exist at 4K granularity
